variables.name: Centripetal Acceleration
variables.description: An acceleration directed toward the center of a circular path.

variables.name: Max Acceleration
variables.description: Maximum acceleration magnitude (always positive).

variables.name: Acceleration
variables.description: The rate of change of velocity.

variables.name: Amplitude
variables.description: Maximum distance from equilibrium (always positive).

variables.name: Change in Momentum
variables.description: The change in an object's momentum during an interaction. It occurs when a force acts over time.

variables.name: Displacement
variables.description: Magnitude of the displacement during motion.

variables.name: Total energy
variables.description: Total mechanical energy of the oscillation (non-negative).

variables.name: Gravitational Potential Energy
variables.description: The potential energy associated with an object's position in a gravitational field.

variables.name: Average Force
variables.description: The average force applied to an object during a time interval. It represents the overall effect of a varying force.

variables.name: Centripetal Force
variables.description: The net force directed toward the center of a circular path in circular motion.

variables.name: Friction Force
variables.description: The force that resists relative motion between two surfaces in contact.

variables.name: Frequency
variables.description: A physical quantity representing the number of revolutions per unit time.

variables.name: Net Force
variables.description: The total force acting on an object.

variables.name: Gravity
variables.description: The acceleration due to gravity near the surface of a planet.

variables.name: Height
variables.description: The vertical distance from a chosen reference level.

variables.name: Impulse
variables.description: The effect of a force acting over a time interval on an object. It represents how motion changes during impacts or interactions.

variables.name: Kinetic energy
variables.description: Energy associated with motion (always non-negative).

variables.name: Spring constant
variables.description: Spring stiffness constant (always positive).

variables.name: Mass
variables.description: A physical quantity representing the amount of matter in an object.

variables.name: Coeff. of Friction
variables.description: A dimensionless quantity representing the ratio between frictional force and normal force.

variables.name: Angular Velocity
variables.description: A physical quantity representing the rate of change of angular position with respect to time.

variables.name: Initial Momentum
variables.description: The momentum of an object before an interaction or applied force. It represents the motion at the beginning of the event.

variables.name: Potential energy
variables.description: Gravitational potential energy relative to a reference level.

variables.name: Pi
variables.description: A mathematical constant representing the ratio of a circle's circumference to its diameter.

variables.name: Momentum
variables.description: The quantity of motion of an object related to its mass and velocity. It appears when describing moving objects and collisions.

variables.name: Power
variables.description: Rate at which work is done or energy is transferred.

variables.name: Radius
variables.description: The distance from the center of a circular path to the moving object.

variables.name: Displacement
variables.description: Displacement from the initial position. The origin is assumed at the starting point (s₀ = 0).

variables.name: Initial Time
variables.description: The time when the force begins acting on the object. It marks the start of the interaction interval.

variables.name: Angle
variables.description: Angle between force and displacement (0–180 degrees).

variables.name: Time Interval
variables.description: The duration of motion.

variables.name: Period
variables.description: The time required for one complete revolution in circular motion.

variables.name: Initial Velocity
variables.description: The velocity of the object at the beginning of the motion.

variables.name: Average Velocity
variables.description: The average rate of displacement over time.

variables.name: Max Velocity
variables.description: Maximum speed during the motion (always positive).

variables.name: Final Velocity
variables.description: The velocity of the object at a later time during the motion.

variables.name: Weight
variables.description: The gravitational force acting on an object.

variables.name: Displacement
variables.description: Position relative to equilibrium (can be positive or negative).
